headline: UBS: AI investment is ‘lone buffer’ for emerging markets as energy costs soar
summary: Investing.com -- Emerging market equities are grappling with a sharp reversal of recent gains as the conflict in the Middle East enters its third week, threatening the growth that has characterized the asset class over the last year. According to a new strategy report from UBS, emerging markets (EM) have become one of the worst-performing asset classes globally since the start of hostilities, as the market shifts into a decisive "risk-off" mode.

content.title: UBS: AI investment is ‘lone buffer’ for emerging markets as energy costs soar

headline: SLB warns of $0.09 EPS hit as Hormuz blockade shuts down Middle East flows
summary: Investing.com -- Global energy services giant Slb NV (NYSE:SLB) is bracing for a hit to its first-quarter earnings as escalating hostilities in the Middle East disrupt regional operations and effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz.

content.title: SLB warns of $0.09 EPS hit as Hormuz blockade shuts down Middle East flows

headline: What Would It Take to Tip the Economy into Recession?
summary: Investing.com – A sustained surge in oil prices could push the U.S. economy into recession if it significantly weakens consumer spending and tightens financial conditions, according to a report from Wells Fargo.

content.title: What Would It Take to Tip the Economy into Recession?
content.summary: Investing.com – A sustained surge in oil prices could push the U.S. economy into recession if it significantly weakens consumer spending and tightens financial conditions, according to a report from Wells Fargo.

headline: How will the oil disruption matter for consumer spending?
summary: Investing.com – Rising oil prices triggered by supply disruptions and geopolitical tensions could begin to weigh on U.S. consumer spending if the shock persists, according to analysts at Morgan Stanley.

content.title: How will the oil disruption matter for consumer spending?
